title: Bleasby (2) W.4232 Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including Plt Off. Bruce Lettington Humphreys

sources.title: Aircrew Remembered - P/O Humphreys, Lancaster W4232

maintainer.notes: Part of the Bleasby Aircrew Memorial site.

external_narratives.summary: Lancaster W4232 (coded RC-Y) of 5 Lancaster Finishing School took off from RAF Syerston at 16:01 on 17 April 1944 on a general training flight. The starboard wing tip detached in flight, the aircraft stalled while climbing, rolled, the port wing detached, and W4232 dived into the ground near the Lock Keeper's Cottage at Hazelford Weir on the River Trent.
key_details: Starboard wing tip detached in flight
Aircraft stalled while climbing and rolled
Crashed near Lock Keeper's Cottage, Hazelford Weir

external_narratives.summary: ASN records the accident of Lancaster Mk I W4232 on 17 April 1944 at Hazelford Lock near RAF Syerston.
The W4232 crew is commemorated on the Bleasby Aircrew Memorial, a stone seat shaped as a Lancaster tail section on Glebe Field, with crew names inscribed on the vertical tail fin surfaces.
key_details: Names inscribed on memorial tail fin surfaces

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
